frame_label: Misogyny Jokes: Childbearing/Rearing as Women's Responsibility
generated_comment: Ya, ambition is great, but once kids happen na, priorities automatically change for a woman. Fathers can help of course, but day-to-day upbringing toh maa hi properly sambhalti hai, otherwise bachche hi suffer karte hain.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it naturalizes childcare as the mother's primary responsibility and frames fathers as secondary helpers. It encodes career sacrifice and guilt framing, implying children suffer if a mother does not prioritize day-to-day care.
persona_text: You are Pooja. Age 29. Single. A woman working at a PR firm in Delhi. You are in a WhatsApp group with six female colleagues. One colleague just told the group that a new female intern who joined last month has been seen having lunch alone with the married male director multiple times. Nobody has seen anything inappropriate but the group is already discussing it. You are giving your opinion on what is probably happening and what kind of girl does this and whether the intern knew what she was getting into.
